title: Define Meshtastic BLE payload-size and ATT behavior contract
description: Why this exists: current Meshtastic apps write one serialized ToRadio protobuf per GATT value and drain one FromRadio protobuf per read, but worst-case payload size depends on ATT MTU, long-write/read behavior, and firmware-generated ToRadio/FromRadio budgets. What needs to be done: determine the exact maximum BLE GATT value sizes LICHEN must accept/emit for Android, iOS, Python, nRF52, and ESP32 Meshtastic compatibility; define failure behavior for oversized ToRadio and chunking/non-chunking assumptions. Acceptance criteria: source refs or captured BLE evidence are recorded; max inbound/outbound payload sizes are explicit; tests or vector Beads are updated to cover boundary cases.
notes: 2026-06-30 defined Meshtastic BLE payload-size and ATT behavior contract in docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md. Source-backed limits from project-LICHEN-t2hn.1: ToRadio_size = 504 bytes, FromRadio_size = 510 bytes, 512-byte characteristic value envelope. Contract: BLE is one raw protobuf per GATT value; no StreamAPI 0x94c3 length prefix and no Meshtastic app-level BLE chunking; ToRadio accepts <=504 and rejects 505+ before decode with no partial state; FromRadio emits <=510 or reduces/splits semantically/errors before GATT; FromNum is 4-byte little-endian queue counter. Linked existing meshtastic_app_compat vectors for StreamAPI prefix rejection and 505-byte ToRadio rejection. Created project-LICHEN-t2hn.21 for missing Android/iOS/Python ATT MTU/long-read/long-write capture evidence at 504/505 and 510/511 boundaries. Reviews: Laplace no findings, noted Zephyr GATT long read/write implementability and no overclaiming because capture evidence is Beaded; Kepler no P0/P1/P2 blockers, confirmed vector alignment and noted stale Rust gatt.rs is outside this diff and already called stale/tracked; Meitner found P2 commit-hygiene issue that t2hn.21 export must be included, fixed by including .beads/issues/project-LICHEN-t2hn.21.json in commit. Verification: git diff --check passed; targeted rg confirmed doc/vectors/Bead references.
close_reason: Defined BLE payload/ATT contract with explicit 504/510 byte limits, deterministic overflow behavior, vector linkage, and ATT evidence follow-up.
